refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and ensure their sum meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s target under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with processing algorithms due to real-time constraints
coupling_check.notes: The two DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize cross-dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk that processing algorithms may not scale with data volume leading to latency breaches
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry performance and processing efficiency; consider fallback or adaptive algorithms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data acquisition and processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary module containing both data acquisition and processing DPs; minimal diffusion.
Vehicle provides raw data but does not process updates; higher diffusion if processing placed here.
Teleoperation module not responsible for worksite model updates; poor containment.
Safety Services unrelated to worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update latency functions within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports tight latency control.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex or overloaded
Interface latency on Network Link could impact overall update latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider splitting processing into submodules if needed; optimize Network Link performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping to acquisition and processing latency requirements.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as both DPs are contained within NGHE Solution module
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end within NGHE Solution using Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine telemetry and network link performance requirements to ensure latency targets are met.
Consider submodule decomposition within NGHE Solution if complexity grows.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized to meet latency targets
open_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module complexity
Network Link latency impacting update timeliness
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition
